title: A couple Reiquarium pictures in more detail
display_title: A couple Reiquarium pictures in more detail
wikitext:
[]: A couple Reiquarium pictures in more detail             Quote Post by someguy » Tue Aug 30, 2011 5:44 pm I was looking through the Reiquarium article and noticed a few images had very sparse descriptions admitting most of the details of the photos weren't recognized. I decided to try using the difference blending mode in Photoshop to compare these images with the clean ones off of the Platinum DVDs and see if I could better tell what was going on (it turns out others have tried this but as far as I can tell I got a better read of what's going on in the picture). The first image I checked was the "Lincoln" image (It's not Lincoln). There are three men in this picture, in a semicircle. A bearded man's head is visible near the upper right of the image. Another man, wearing glasses, is about a third of the image to the left - his arm is extended towards a round object below the bearded man. A third man is shown in profile in the bottom left - he also has his arm (wristwatch visible) reaching under the bearded man's head. The second image had an even more vague description of a man looking down. There are four men in this picture, standing around a large piece of paper laid out flat. Their heads form a diagonal line starting just to the right of the upper left corner. Two of them have their hands resting on the paper. The rightmost man is most detailed, wearing a jacket, his arm extended towards the paper. There appears to be scaffolding in the background. This image is almost certainly a photograph. -- Hopefully this isn't old news that just hadn't made it to the article and I haven't wasted my time :P

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 30, 2011 6:06 pm I think the article was taken from this thread from ANF back in 2005: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3782 Dunno if there's any more info in that thread.

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Tue Aug 30, 2011 6:38 pm Yeah, thanks, that was where I saw they used the difference effect. I skimmed that thread to see if anyone had better identified these pictures and didn't catch one but I could always have missed something

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Aug 30, 2011 6:47 pm I can't seem to help but see something else in that second Reiquarium picture: http://i.imgur.com/SFOzA.gif

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Aug 30, 2011 6:50 pm Thank you, that's the best result I've seen at enhancing these images. Did you happen to try your luck with this one?: [wikimg width=500:25k9od06]File:Tank image 22.jpg[/wikimg:25k9od06] We were never able to make out any details at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Aug 30, 2011 6:54 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Aug 30, 2011 7:34 pm esselfortium wrote: I can't seem to help but see something else in that second Reiquarium picture

[]: Re: A couple Reiquarium pictures in more detail             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 30, 2011 8:17 pm someguy wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Tue Aug 30, 2011 9:04 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: You're definitely getting better results with your method.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Aug 30, 2011 9:20 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Wed Aug 31, 2011 12:19 am Rei'S! Rei'S EVERYWHERE!

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Wed Aug 31, 2011 5:18 am Hmm, interesting theory, but I don't see Lilith or Adam in that picture. I do notice the objects that might be cranes. I feel like I'm on the verge of seeing it but not yet. I figured out an alternative way to blend the images which I used for this next image, but I will try it for that one tomorrow. [URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/827/23c317npc30bigb.png/] [/URL] I believe this is a white blood cell (or t-cell) along the endothelium /artery wall, or maybe (and more appropriately I guess) an egg cell , although I'm no biologist so I could be way off. The image with the overlay has horrible distortion artifacts in it. If you look very closely you can see where parts of the image have sheared. I'm not sure if this is a capture problem or the tank originally had a rippling water effect but the net result is it is physically impossible to align both images perfectly.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Aug 31, 2011 7:58 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Aug 31, 2011 8:48 am Could it be a skyshot of a building? It looks like a science-y building, could be a lab building or something near the excavation site.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 31, 2011 11:17 am No real improvement, more loss of detail. Horizontal shift motion filter -> alpha, compensated by overlaying 6 different stills of the scene. Problem is, the overlay itself is moving vertically so it gets a little blurred.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Wed Aug 31, 2011 12:05 pm I see a beautiful butterfly floating gently over a cheese grater wearing a sweater vest.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Aug 31, 2011 12:47 pm someguy wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 31, 2011 4:02 pm Anyone still have a clip of this version...?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Aug 31, 2011 6:23 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Aug 31, 2011 6:31 pm top right = cabin in the woods, snow on the ground. bottom = one or more guys hunkered down behind a log fence (possibly a bridge over a shallow creekbed?), either hiding or examining something. left = guy in combat gear using some sort of periscope to look across the scene from behind cover.
